terms.name: Barbault cyclical index
terms.plain: A number measuring how spread apart or clustered the 5 slowest planets are. When planets cluster (low index), historical crises concentrate. When they disperse (high index), recovery and expansion follow.
terms.example: Index hit its lowest 21st-century point in 2020 — right when COVID emerged. André Barbault predicted a pandemic-scale event for 2020 in a 2011 publication.

terms.name: Conjunction
terms.plain: When two planets occupy the same zodiacal longitude (0° separation). For slow outer planets, conjunctions mark the start of multi-decade cycles. Each planet pair has a characteristic period and theme.
terms.example: Jupiter-Saturn conjunction in Aquarius (Dec 2020) — the 'Great Conjunction' that inaugurated the Air Era. Previous Air Era: 1186-1405 CE (medieval trade revolution).

terms.name: Planetary cycle
terms.plain: The time between consecutive conjunctions of two planets. Faster pairs (Jupiter-Saturn: 20 years) set political rhythms. Slower pairs (Neptune-Pluto: 492 years) mark civilizational epochs.
terms.example: Saturn-Pluto cycle (33 years): 1914 → 1947 → 1982 → 2020. Each conjunction restructured global power. The 2020 conjunction coincided with pandemic lockdowns worldwide.

terms.name: Ephemeris
terms.plain: A table of calculated planetary positions for each day (or year). The computational backbone behind the Barbault Index. Modern ephemerides (JPL DE441) are accurate to arcseconds over millennia.
terms.example: The Swiss Ephemeris (used by most astrology software) is based on NASA JPL data. Our Barbault data uses published ephemeris calculations for outer planet longitudes.

terms.name: Asabiyyah
terms.plain: Ibn Khaldun's key concept: the group solidarity or collective will that allows a society to act as one. High asabiyyah enables conquest and state-building. It decays naturally over ~4 generations as luxury replaces hardship.
terms.example: Early American Republic had high asabiyyah (shared revolutionary identity). Current polling shows institutional trust at 22% — a textbook late-cycle asabiyyah reading.

terms.name: Political Stress Index (PSI)
terms.plain: Peter Turchin's quantitative measure combining mass mobilization potential (MMP), elite competition (EMP), and state fiscal distress (SFD). Values above 0.8 historically precede political violence or revolution.
terms.example: US PSI peaked around 1970 (Vietnam/civil rights unrest) then declined. It has been rising since ~2000 and now exceeds the 1970 peak — the highest recorded level.

terms.name: Elite overproduction
terms.plain: When a society produces more elite aspirants (lawyers, MBAs, PhDs, political candidates) than elite positions available. The frustrated surplus becomes counter-elite, funding and leading revolutionary movements.
terms.example: US lawyer/population ratio at historical peak. PhD production 3x 1970 levels while tenure-track positions declined 70%. This surplus drives political radicalization on both left and right.

terms.name: Structural-Demographic Theory
terms.plain: Turchin and Goldstone's framework: political instability results from the interaction of population growth, elite overproduction, and state fiscal crisis. These three factors reinforce each other in ~50-year 'fathers and sons' cycles within longer ~200-300 year secular cycles.
terms.example: The model predicted in 2010 (published in Nature) that US political instability would peak around 2020. This was before Trump, COVID, or January 6th.

terms.name: Kondratieff wave
terms.plain: Named after Soviet economist Nikolai Kondratieff (executed by Stalin for this theory). Long waves of ~45-60 years driven by technology adoption: Spring (growth), Summer (inflation), Autumn (financialization), Winter (depression/restructuring).
terms.example: 5th K-wave (Information Age) Winter began with 2008 crisis. We're now in the transition to the 6th wave (AI/Bio), similar to the 1930s-1940s transition from 3rd to 4th wave.

terms.name: Carlota Perez framework
terms.plain: Venezuelan economist's refinement of K-waves. Each tech revolution has an Installation period (financial capital leads, ending in bubble/crash) and a Deployment period (production capital leads, 'Golden Age'). The 'Turning Point' between them requires institutional adaptation.
terms.example: Dot-com crash (2000) and 2008 GFC were the Turning Point crash of the Information Age. We're waiting for the institutional recomposition that enables the Golden Age — historically this takes a major policy shift (like the New Deal).

terms.name: Fourth Turning
terms.plain: The final phase of the Strauss-Howe generational cycle (~80-year saeculum). A period of existential crisis where civic institutions are torn down and rebuilt. The old social order is destroyed and replaced.
terms.example: Previous Fourth Turnings: American Revolution (1773-1794), Civil War (1860-1865), Depression/WWII (1929-1946). Current: 2008-~2033. The climax is projected for 2025-2030.

terms.name: Saeculum
terms.plain: From Latin 'a long human life' (~80 years). The full cycle of four turnings (High → Awakening → Unraveling → Crisis). Each saeculum ends with a Crisis that resets institutional life.
terms.example: Current Millennial saeculum began 1946 (post-WWII High). Four turnings: 1946-1964 (High), 1964-1984 (Awakening), 1984-2008 (Unraveling), 2008-~2033 (Crisis).

terms.name: Dalio Big Cycle
terms.plain: Ray Dalio's model of how reserve-currency empires rise and fall through 6 stages: from New Order through prosperity to overextension, internal conflict, and Great Disorder. Driven by debt, education, innovation, and internal cohesion cycles.
terms.example: Dutch Empire (1600-1780), British Empire (1780-1945), American Era (1945-?). Each transition involved the rising power's currency displacing the declining power's. China's yuan vs US dollar is the current contest.

terms.name: Power Index
terms.plain: Dalio's composite of 8 factors: education, innovation, trade, output, military, financial center status, reserve currency status, and resource allocation. Scored 0-1 for each nation. The crossing point where a rising power exceeds the declining hegemon historically triggers the 'Thucydides Trap.'
terms.example: US: 0.82 (declining from ~0.95 in 1950). China: 0.73 (rising from ~0.2 in 1980). Crossing projected 2035-2045 on current trajectories, but not inevitable.

terms.name: As Above, So Below
terms.plain: The Hermetic principle that patterns at one scale reflect patterns at another. In this system: celestial cycles, demographic cycles, economic cycles, and generational cycles all showing crisis convergence in the 2020s is treated as meaningful correspondence, not coincidence.
terms.example: 2020: Barbault minimum + PSI peak + K-wave winter + Fourth Turning + Dalio Stage 5. Five independent frameworks from five different traditions all pointing to the same decade. Coincidence or correspondence?

terms.name: Yuga
terms.plain: Vedic time division. Sri Yukteswar's calibration: ascending arc of yugas (Kali → Dwapara → Treta → Satya), each ~2,400 years. We entered ascending Dwapara Yuga (~1700 CE), the 'energy age' — humanity learning to perceive and manipulate energy directly.
terms.example: Ascending Dwapara began ~1700: electricity discovered, atomic energy, now AI. Each century brings mastery of subtler forces. Matches the Kondratieff progression from mechanical to electrical to informational to AI.

terms.name: Jiazi
terms.plain: The Chinese 60-year sexagenary cycle combining 10 Heavenly Stems and 12 Earthly Branches. Each Jiazi marks an institutional reset. Used in Chinese cosmology, medicine, and strategic planning for millennia.
terms.example: New Jiazi began 2024 (Wood Dragon). Previous: 1964 (Cultural Revolution, Civil Rights Act, ARPANET conception), 1904 (Qing Dynasty collapse, Wright Brothers, Einstein). Each inaugurated a new relationship with a fundamental force.

terms.name: Creative Destruction
terms.plain: Schumpeter's concept: capitalism advances by destroying old industries to birth new ones. The K-wave Winter is peak creative destruction — old paradigm firms die, new paradigm firms emerge. Pain is the mechanism, not a bug.
terms.example: Current: AI destroying traditional knowledge work (legal research, coding, writing, analysis) while creating new paradigms. Previous: automobiles destroying horse-economy (farriers, stable hands, carriage makers) in K-wave 4 Spring.

terms.name: Saturn-Neptune conjunction
terms.plain: The 36-year cycle where Saturn (structure, reality) meets Neptune (ideals, illusion). Each conjunction dissolves a dominant ideology and seeds its replacement. The most politically predictive of all planetary cycles.
terms.example: 1917: Tsarism dissolved, Communism born. 1953: Stalin died, Cold War ideology shifted. 1989: Communism dissolved, liberal democratic 'End of History' born. 2026: Liberal internationalism dissolving — what replaces it?

terms.name: North Node
terms.plain: The point where the Moon's orbit crosses the ecliptic heading north. In astrology, it represents your growth direction — the qualities and life areas you're developing toward. It moves backward through the zodiac on an 18.6-year cycle.
terms.example: A North Node in Aquarius suggests growth through innovation, collective service, and independent thinking — moving away from South Node Leo patterns of ego-centered self-expression.

terms.name: South Node
terms.plain: Always exactly opposite the North Node. Represents ingrained patterns, comfort zones, and past tendencies. Growth comes from moving toward North Node qualities while integrating (not abandoning) South Node strengths.
terms.example: South Node in Leo with North Node in Aquarius: natural leadership and self-expression are your foundation, but growth requires channeling these gifts toward collective rather than personal goals.

terms.name: Natal chart
terms.plain: A map of planetary positions at your exact moment of birth, projected onto the zodiac and divided into 12 houses representing life areas. The North/South Node axis is the most growth-oriented element — it shows where you're coming from and where you're heading.
terms.example: Your natal chart is computed from three inputs: date, time, and location of birth. The time determines which zodiac sign was rising on the eastern horizon (your Ascendant), which sets all 12 house positions.
